methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $150M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Einride
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Harbinger
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
ZeroAvia is a Series C stage transportation company that has raised $150M+. The broader Transportation sector comprises 7 private comparables, including peers such as Einride, Harbinger, Rivian, The Boring Company, and REGENT Craft, with no established public trading comps available for direct benchmarking.

The valuation analysis relies on a stage-progression methodology, which applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to the $150M total raised proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $255M. A peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient narrow-stage comparables within Transportation—while 5 same-sector peers exist, fewer than 3 qualified within same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation-tier filters, making sector-wide median benchmarking unreliable.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $217M (low) to $293M (high), with a median of $255M. The single-method foundation introduces material uncertainty around this estimate. Key sensitivities include the appropriateness of the 1.7x multiplier for ZeroAvia's specific capital efficiency and market traction relative to generalized Series C dynamics, and the continued absence of public comparables limits confidence in long-term exit multiples. Additional validation through revenue or unit economics data, if available, would materially strengthen the range.
